title: lepidagathis stenophylla
intro: Lepidagathis stenophylla, also known as the 'Panicled Lepidagathis' or 'Indian Sage', is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Acanthaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy in the region. In traditional herbal preparations, Lepidagathis stenophylla is often used as a decoction or infusion, or combined with other herbs to create a medicinal tea or poultice.
